verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase resident in the cytoplasm and associated with intracellular membrane compartments (inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal lipidation, and various endomembranes). It lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any mechanism to expose a protein body on the extracellular face. The only potential surface story is pMHC presentation of SRC-derived peptides on intracellular cells (relevant for TCR-T / TCR-mimic programs), but pMHC presentation alone does not establish surface accessibility of the protein body itself and is explicitly excluded from this triage per scope.
